126696.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women. Such a government represents all perspectives to make well-considered decisions.

126697.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. A referendum as well as a citizens’ council can be a good way to send a decision back to the municipal council and request a new proposal.

126700.explanation: Agriculture must become much more sustainable, but we also want farmers to make money with their businesses. A shop with good products from their own land or a small campsite can help with that. Therefore, we want to enable these options.

126703.explanation: Those who bring workers to our municipality for employment must ensure these workers have decent housing. Without causing inconvenience for our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in Waterland.

126704.explanation: The Netherlands is a crowded country and we must protect the green spaces we have. Greenery is vital for cooling, clean air, and a pleasant neighborhood. Animals and other plants cannot live without trees. That is why we have successfully proposed that for every 9 trees cut down, 10 must be replanted.

126706.explanation: You celebrate New Year’s together, with a fireworks show or a light show. For years, we have proposed a fireworks ban due to nuisance to people and animals. Fireworks are used for vandalism against emergency services. We choose a safe and pleasant New Year’s Eve.

126707.explanation: During festivals and events, the sound is often heard throughout much of the municipality. The noise also causes hearing damage and disturbs birds and other animals. Therefore, the sound levels at festivals and events should be reduced.

126708.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are still not properly accessible, for example for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adjustments on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality of Waterland should provide support.

126709.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage and an additional home in a yard can be a good solution. For example, as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We want to make this possible for farmers who want it. We do not allow building in the meadow or in nature.

126710.explanation: Sports provide a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people receiving social assistance benefits, sports are often too expensive. As far as we are concerned, the municipality of Waterland should ensure this group can participate in sports clubs for free.

126713.explanation: As a child, you are under enormous pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons to be accessible not only for children with wealthy parents. This way, every child gets a chance to experience music in life.

126714.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

126715.explanation: Our monuments make Waterland beautiful and unique. We believe they must be well cared for. It would be a huge pity if unique places are lost forever because we choose to cut budgets now.

126717.explanation: The enforcement officers (BOA’s), together with the police and fire brigade, are important for safety in Waterland. The BOA’s should be deployed purposefully to reduce nuisance, for example by enforcing rules against fireworks nuisance.

126718.explanation: Expansion is desired in places that are unsafe. For our privacy, we do not want a society with “a camera on every corner.” We prefer to have more enforcement officers on the streets who can intervene immediately if things go wrong.

126721.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

126722.explanation: We want to protect the open landscape. In Waterland, more than 1,000 affordable homes are being built in the coming years. New plans are also arriving every year in the village centers and in Monnickendam.

126723.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they do not want to work, but because they do not meet employers’ requirements. Often they are volunteers or informal caregivers. That is useful and only works well if people do it voluntarily. Forcing them has the opposite effect and causes new problems.

126725.explanation: People with an income just above the threshold often struggle financially. By raising the income limit, we help more Waterlanders. For example, we chose this approach for help with energy allowances. This prevents debts, which saves money for the municipality.

126727.explanation: If a neighborhood has too few charging points for electric cars, more parking spaces with a charging station must be added. If there are already unused charging points in a district, no new ones need to be added yet.

126729.explanation: Freedom of choice is very important to us. This also includes the choice of healthcare provider.

126730.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You do not solve this by cutting more budget and always choosing the cheapest option. Always opting for the cheapest provider means necessary but complex care is compromised, which is undesirable.

126732.explanation: For dogs, the municipality provides poop bags and trash bins in places where dogs are walked. This reduces nuisance caused by dog waste on the streets.

126733.explanation: We want to increase the tourist tax and use it to continue funding amenities. It is fair that tourists pay their share of the costs in the municipality. Raising the tourist tax is not an end in itself.

126734.explanation: A large new business park is being developed in Edam-Volendam, and there is still space on business parks in Purmerend. Therefore, in Waterland we can keep the meadows green.

126735.explanation: We find it important that shops can open on Sundays for shop owners and customers who want it. This also makes it much easier for singles who work during the week to do important shopping. In Waterland, shops are already allowed to open on Sundays.

126736.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are allowed to stay in the Netherlands but cannot easily find housing themselves. The municipality is required to ensure they find housing. In Waterland, of the more than 100 social rental homes per year, fewer than 10 go to status holders.

126743.explanation: A survey by the mayor found there are no suitable locations for a coffeeshop in Waterland. A coffeeshop next to a school is not a good idea.

126744.explanation: In Waterland, we successfully proposed that in any new construction project 30% social rental homes and 30% affordable homes (purchase and rental) must be built. Now it is important to start building these homes as soon as possible.

126746.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. That is why we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented for a fair price.

126748.explanation: There are already sufficient parking spaces in the municipality of Waterland. More parking spaces mean green areas must disappear. In addition, we are building a sports hall with many parking spaces underneath. These parking spots can also be used outside of sports hours.

126750.explanation: In Waterland, we already separate waste. That is cheaper and more sustainable. For Waterlanders with a small or no garden, we provide underground containers in every neighborhood.

126751.explanation: Paid parking in the center of Monnickendam is an option if the blue zone does not have the desired effect. By introducing paid parking, visitors pay instead of residents. Paid parking also reduces car traffic in the city center.

126752.explanation: Sport is healthy. If all Waterlanders exercise more, healthcare costs will drop significantly and Waterlanders’ quality of life will improve. Investing in good sports facilities is an investment that pays for itself.

126753.explanation: Sharing canteens and sports fields fosters greater connection among Waterlanders.

126696.explanation: What matters is having the best possible candidates! It's not about the numbers of different groups.

126697.explanation: No, there are better ways to give your opinion than just referenda. For example, a permanent digital participation platform where every resident can easily share their opinion. Also: are we talking about advisory referenda or binding referenda?

126700.explanation: No, that could have too much impact on our beautiful landscape. We are proud of our landscape as heritage. We therefore handle it very carefully.

126703.explanation: No, sometimes there are better shelter options outside the entrepreneur’s own site. That is more important than forcing entrepreneurs to house people on their own premises.

126704.explanation: Excellent idea. We support the “11 trees back for every 10 removed” policy as is current in this municipality.

126706.explanation: If the municipality does not organize a fireworks show itself, it should support neighborhood or village initiatives that do.

126707.explanation: There is currently no need in our municipality to intervene in the noise standards at festivals.

126708.explanation: No, entrepreneurs must ensure accessibility themselves. This applies both to their own staff and to visitors.

126709.explanation: In some cases, this should be possible. Damage to the landscape must, of course, be limited.

126710.explanation: There are already several poverty schemes in this municipality. Support for sports memberships already exists in some form. A new scheme does not seem necessary.

126713.explanation: We are very pleased with the Music School. It is a “luxury” option, especially compared to, for example, swimming lessons and a swimming pool.

126714.explanation: The municipality should not be required to contribute to struggling sports associations that have almost no active members left.

126715.explanation: The monuments in this municipality are something to be proud of. They attract many visitors. We must take good care of them!

126717.explanation: Enforcement is a neglected area in the municipality of Waterland. More budget should be allocated here. Nuisance is hardly enforced at all.

126718.explanation: After privacy risks have been carefully assessed according to the GDPR, extra camera surveillance should be possible in places where a lot of nuisance occurs.

126721.explanation: The municipality must be able to allow exceptions to the main rule that permanent residence is not permitted. For example, in urgent cases.

126722.explanation: It should be possible to add small numbers of houses on the outer edge of existing centers. Obviously, we should not just build over the meadows. We are careful with our landscape. The idea of "adding a small street" should remain possible.

126723.explanation: The municipality may require a counter-performance from recipients of social assistance.

126725.explanation: The municipality pursues a generous social policy. We are proud of that. If the municipality is no longer able to finance this, difficult choices will have to be made.

126727.explanation: The number of charging points should organically grow with the number of electric cars. Charging spots should not already be taken from petrol drivers.

126729.explanation: No, good care is provided by care providers. There should be no "shopping around" within the generous care offer.

126730.explanation: The focus should especially be on quality and thus on results. Something more expensive, but with better results, ultimately leads to lower costs in youth care.

126732.explanation: Dogs cause waste and nuisance. This must be cleaned up and therefore paid for.

126733.explanation: The tourist tax can be increased in phases; other forms of tourist tax should be open for discussion.

126734.explanation: Unfortunately, there are almost no business parks left in Waterland. Expansion possibilities per company must be reviewed. In some cases this is possible and feasible, in others not. Economic policy must be developed for this.

126735.explanation: The municipality should develop a vision on the economy and retail. Opening hours should be included in this. We have no objections to weekend or evening openings.

126736.explanation: No, this causes the youth of Waterland to move away because they find housing outside the municipality. Or they continue living with their parents for a long time. We do not consider that a healthy situation.

126743.explanation: The municipality can develop a policy in the future that makes it possible to establish a coffeeshop. For now, there are no possibilities for this in our municipality due to the high risk of nuisance.

126744.explanation: The municipal policy already distributes 30% social rental, 30% mid-rent and social owner-occupied, and 40% free sector. The share of social rental does not need to increase further.

126746.explanation: No, this causes nuisance for neighbors and possibly exploitation and/or undermining of the municipality. This is evident, for example, from Amsterdam where this form of rental was allowed for a while and is now restricted again.

126748.explanation: There should be more free parking spaces just around the centers of the towns. Not more spaces in the centers themselves.

126750.explanation: Residents already have to separate their waste now. We are against this. Post-separation by the waste processor yields much better results and is more customer-friendly.

126751.explanation: In the long term, temporary or partially paid parking in the center for visitors can be considered. First, the results of the new parking policy must be clear before new parking measures are taken.

126752.explanation: It is not wise to start with an amount first and then prepare a plan. Sportpark Markgouw must be rearranged and overdue maintenance addressed. Whether more or less than 1 million euros should be spent must first be investigated.

126753.explanation: Own identity. More efficient use of fields and buildings must be considered in the desired redesign of Sportpark Markgouw.

126696.explanation: The CDA emphasizes quality, cooperation, and trust. A mandatory choice for an equal number of men and women does not fit with that.

126697.explanation: The CDA program emphasizes participation and input. A referendum, with risks such as low turnout, extra costs, and complex issues not suitable for yes/no questions, is not preferred. Moreover, weighing interests is ideally the role of the municipal council.

126700.explanation: Diversified agriculture is supported, but always appropriate to the environment and permits.

126703.explanation: "Agree" with the explanation: "This does not occur in Waterland, but if it did, the CDA supports housing on own grounds to avoid extra pressure on the local housing market." "Disagree" with the explanation: "Too much dependence on one employer is not in the interest of seasonal workers and can lead to exploitation."

126704.explanation: The CDA’s program focuses on more greenery and sustainable management of public spaces.

126706.explanation: There is a national ban. In the coming years it will become clear how residents deal with this new situation. Private initiatives are welcomed, but the CDA does not yet see this as a municipal task.

126707.explanation: This is about balancing livability and community life. Moreover, noise standards already apply.

126708.explanation: The CDA program emphasizes the importance of accessibility for people with disabilities; (financial) incentives for entrepreneurs are one option.

126709.explanation: Provincial and municipal policy provide the space where building is permitted.

126710.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in sports, regardless of income.

126713.explanation: Cultural education, including music, for children is important to the CDA and contributes to a healthy society.

126714.explanation: One of the priorities in the CDA program is the preservation and strengthening of sports clubs.

126715.explanation: The CDA program emphasizes care for heritage and sustainability. Cutting back on monument care does not fit with that.

126717.explanation: The CDA is in favor of more (visible) enforcement.

126718.explanation: Safety is important. Camera surveillance only in exceptional situations.

126721.explanation: Each situation can be assessed to determine if permanent residence in holiday homes is possible.

126722.explanation: Housing development at the edges of existing centers contributes to the necessary construction and promotes the livability of the respective center.

126723.explanation: Stimulating participation is important, but the CDA is not in favor of making it mandatory.

126725.explanation: The CDA program wants broader access to support for poverty.

126727.explanation: The CDA program promotes electric transport and related facilities.

126729.explanation: Care must be humane and appropriate.

126730.explanation: In youth care, quality and cooperation are considered more important than just costs.

126732.explanation: There is insufficient reason to maintain the dog tax. Moreover, the net revenue is limited and the municipality does not provide a reciprocal service.

126733.explanation: The CDA expects tourists to make a fair contribution to livability, maintenance level, and facilities in the municipality.

126734.explanation: The CDA wants to provide space for entrepreneurship, but fitting the scale of Waterland.

126735.explanation: The CDA is in favor of maintaining the current situation.

126736.explanation: The municipality is obliged to implement national policy.

126743.explanation: The CDA sees no necessity and is therefore not in favor of establishing a coffeeshop in Waterland.

126744.explanation: The CDA supports the current municipal policy in which 30% social housing is included.

126746.explanation: Room rental can contribute to solving housing shortages for, for example, students.

126748.explanation: Accessibility and sufficient parking are important for all towns and neighborhoods.

126750.explanation: The CDA is in favor of waste separation.

126751.explanation: Paid parking will only be considered if there is sufficient support.

126752.explanation: The CDA wants investments in Sports Park Markgouw to maintain and strengthen sports facilities; it is unknown what costs may be involved.

126753.explanation: The CDA program encourages cooperation and shared use of (sports) facilities.

126696.explanation: VVD Waterland looks only at the qualities of individual candidates when appointing mayors and aldermen, not at their gender.

126697.explanation: Sometimes decisions are so fundamental that they should not be made with an ordinary voter mandate.

126700.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126703.explanation: This does not occur in our municipality.

126704.explanation: Waterland is a green municipality and must remain so. Trees are important, but it is not necessary as an automatic rule to plant a new one for every tree cut down.

126706.explanation: Our municipality consists of a large number of towns. VVD Waterland is not in favor of one municipal fireworks show, which would probably take place in Monnickendam.

126709.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126710.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126715.explanation: The amount the municipality currently has available for monument care is already not high, so cutting this is unrealistic.

126717.explanation: VVD Waterland finds it important that there is more visible police presence in our municipality. It is not a good idea to (further) delegate security tasks to enforcement officers (BOAs).

126718.explanation: VVD Waterland considers the privacy of residents very important and is therefore against mass surveillance by the municipality through cameras.

126721.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126722.explanation: A good example is the Ilpenstein plan in Ilpendam. VVD Waterland strongly supports this. That does not mean all green spaces should be built over. For example, we oppose building over the Purmer.

126723.explanation: It is very important for people to be socially engaged. Additionally, it is not unreasonable to request a reciprocal effort from people receiving social assistance.

126730.explanation: The municipality must pay attention not only to the price but especially to the quality of care.

126732.explanation: Principally, it is strange to tax a dog. There is no tax on cats or guinea pigs either.

126733.explanation: Tourist tax has increased significantly in recent years and should not increase further. It must be considered that every euro paid in this tax comes at the expense of Waterland hotel owners or residents renting a B&B.

126734.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126743.explanation: As long as soft drugs are not legalized in the Netherlands, VVD Waterland is not in favor of establishing a coffeeshop.

126748.explanation: It is important that there is sufficient parking availability.

126750.explanation: VVD Waterland wants to reverse excessive waste separation. So: just a gray and green bin, with weekly collection frequency.

126751.explanation: Paid parking for visitors will mean that residents must also pay. They will have to purchase a parking permit annually from the municipality.

126752.explanation: Sports facilities are important. It is better to invest in renewing Sports Park Markgouw than to spend more than €22 million on building a megalomaniac sports hall in Monnickendam (where certainly few people in other towns are waiting for it).

126753.explanation: This depends very much on the specific situation.

126696.explanation: It would be a desirable situation but quality always comes first.

126697.explanation: A referendum is not necessary in our municipality because residents can participate via local councils and citizen initiatives.

126700.explanation: This option must certainly be kept open.

126703.explanation: Employers should provide housing, but it does not necessarily have to be on their own company premises.

126704.explanation: Preferably even a bit more trees.

126706.explanation: A fireworks show should be organized by the residents. The municipality has a facilitating role.

126707.explanation: This is already arranged when applying for an event permit.

126708.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality but for the entrepreneur themselves.

126709.explanation: As long as it fits within the Environmental Regulation.

126710.explanation: This is already arranged in our municipality's poverty policy.

126713.explanation: We support the Waterland music school and will continue to do so.

126714.explanation: Sports clubs must be self-sufficient; the municipality has a facilitating role (e.g., management of sports fields).

126715.explanation: The monuments are the gems within our municipality.

126717.explanation: But we see the role of enforcement officers (boa's) as much broader than safety (e.g., traffic).

126718.explanation: Camera surveillance must be used sparingly, with privacy in mind.

126721.explanation: With the current housing crisis, this must be possible.

126722.explanation: Preference is for inner-city locations or 'just one more street'.

126725.explanation: The municipality of Waterland is a social municipality.

126727.explanation: Currently, there are sufficient charging points for electric cars. Future developments should be monitored.

126729.explanation: Not a task for the municipal government.

126730.explanation: Price is an important aspect, but quality and duration of treatment must be monitored.

126732.explanation: This tax is currently not used for facilities.

126733.explanation: Tourist tax must be in accordance with the tax in surrounding municipalities.

126734.explanation: Local entrepreneurs must be able to develop.

126736.explanation: The current arrangement (5% of social rental housing allocated to refugees) works well.

126743.explanation: We want to separate the sale of hard and soft drugs. A coffee shop regulates the sale of soft drugs.

126744.explanation: 30% of the new homes must be social housing.

126746.explanation: There must be the possibility to split houses.

126748.explanation: More bicycle parking spaces are needed.

126751.explanation: Is a better option than the blue zone.

126752.explanation: An investigation must be conducted into costs and location.

statements.title: Waterland should introduce paid parking for visitors in the city centre of Monnickendam.
Business parks in Waterland should be allowed to expand.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Waterland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Waterland should introduce a system for residents to separate their waste.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: When redesigning Sportpark Markgouw, sports clubs should share canteens and sports fields.

titleParts.glossary: The redesign or alteration of a street or square. For example: different paving, new plants and trees, or traffic measures like speed bumps.

titleParts.text: van Sportpark Markgouw moeten sportverenigingen samen gebruikmaken van kantines en sportvelden.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The residents of Waterland should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Waterland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More camera surveillance should be installed in Waterland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should spend more than 1 million euros on renewing Sportpark Markgouw.

shootoutStatements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.
The tourist tax should be increased.
At least 30% of the new homes to be built in Waterland must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.
